_meta.name: FINARODA Concept Tooltip Content
_meta.source: FINARODA internal guide (personal tool v25.83), curated by mentor session. Definitions are educational, descriptive, never prescriptive.
rules: No em dashes anywhere, use commas or colons (Nadav copy rule, 12/07).

Structure per term: what (one plain-language line, max ~160 chars), now (contextual template, placeholders in {braces} filled by the implementation from the current scan/episode), academy (module id).
Language: simple English, no jargon inside a definition unless that jargon has its own tooltip.
Never advice: describe what a value means, never what the user should do.

ema200.what: The average closing price of the last 200 days, weighted toward recent days. It is the widely watched line between a healthy market and a damaged one.
ema200.now: Price is {distance_pct}% {above_below} the 200-day average. {above: 'The long-term structure supports strength.' | below: 'The long-term structure is negative, most rallies in this state have failed historically.'}

weekly_bias.what: The direction of the weekly chart, one candle per week. It answers: is the big picture rising or falling, regardless of today's noise?
weekly_bias.now: The weekly structure is {bullish_bearish}. Daily moves against it are swimming upstream.

ema7.what: A fast average of the last 7 days. Price hugging it means an orderly trend, price far from it means stretched and likely to snap back.
ema7.now: Price is {distance_pct}% from the 7-day average. {near: 'Close to the line: orderly.' | far: 'Stretched: entries far from the line have performed worse in our data.'}

ema7_slope.what: How steeply the 7-day average is rising or falling. Our single most validated timing signal: it beat random entries in 78.7% of tests, on all 10 coins, across 4 years of data.
ema7_slope.now: Slope is {slope_pct}% ({direction}). {aligned: 'Slope agrees with the setup direction.' | against: 'Slope disagrees with the setup direction, historically weaker timing.'}

ema_general.what: A line that smooths price into its recent average, giving more weight to newer days. Fast averages (7 days) track mood, slow ones (200 days) track structure.

closed_candle.what: A candle that has finished its full time period. We score only closed daily candles: a candle still forming can change its mind, a closed one cannot.
closed_candle.now: The score is computed on the last closed daily candle (00:00 UTC). It stays fixed until the next candle closes, only the live timing layer updates during the day.

score.what: A 0 to 100 grade of how many of the methodology's 11 checks the coin passes right now, weighted by importance. It measures conditions, it does not predict.
score.now: This coin scores {score}. The threshold for PASS is 85, identical on every plan.

pass_watch.what: PASS (85 or higher): the methodology's conditions are met. WATCH (82 to 84): close but not there, shown for observation only.
pass_watch.now: {status}: score {score}. {pass: 'Conditions met.' | watch: 'Observation only, not counted as a setup.'}

methodology_conditions.what: Eleven independent checks on structure, momentum, volume and positioning. Two are mandatory: the 200-day regime and the weekly structure. Fail those and nothing passes.
methodology_conditions.now: {passed_count} of 11 checks passed. {blocked: 'A mandatory check failed, so no score can produce a PASS today.'}

no_setups.what: Today's honest answer. Most days the market offers nothing that clears the bar, and not acting on those days is where most capital is saved.
no_setups.now: {scanned_count} coins scanned, none cleared 85. This result is recorded in your journal like any other.

smart_skip.what: Choosing not to act because conditions were not met. In our recorded data, skipped days repeatedly avoided drawdowns. Skipping is a decision, and it is measured.
smart_skip.now: Your journal tracks what skipped setups would have done, so discipline becomes visible instead of invisible.

rsi.what: A 0 to 100 gauge of how hard price has been pushed recently. High readings mean stretched up, low readings mean stretched down.

rsi_momentum.what: For an upward setup we want RSI 55 or higher, for a downward one 45 or lower: momentum should already lean in the setup's direction.

rsi_extreme.what: Above 80 or below 20 the move is usually exhausted. The score is penalized at these extremes because entries there chase a move that already happened.
rsi_extreme.now: RSI {rsi_value} is in the extreme zone, the score carries a penalty.

atr.what: The coin's typical daily movement in price terms. It calibrates everything to the coin's own volatility: a 2% move is noise for one coin and an event for another.
atr.now: ATR is {atr_value} ({atr_pct}% of price). Risk levels are placed using this, not a fixed distance.

adx_trend.what: Measures how strongly price is trending, in any direction. Below 20: drifting sideways. Above 25: a real trend is in motion.

fvg.what: A price zone the market jumped over so fast that almost no trading happened there. Price often returns to fill these gaps, so an open gap in your direction is a supportive sign.
fvg.now: {present: 'An open daily gap exists in the setup direction at {fvg_zone}.' | absent: 'No open gap in the setup direction.'}

wick_rejection.what: A long candle wick shows price tried to go somewhere and was pushed back. Repeated long wicks against a setup mean sellers or buyers are actively defending that area.
wick_rejection.now: {clean: 'Recent candles show no heavy rejection against the setup.' | dirty: 'Recent candles show rejection wicks against the setup.'}

ltf_cross.what: When the 7-day average crosses the 14-day average, the short-term tide has turned. We read it on closed daily candles only.
ltf_cross.now: {crossed: 'The 7-day average has crossed the 14-day in the setup direction.' | not: 'No supporting cross yet.'}

swing_levels.what: Prices where the market previously turned. Buyers defend old lows (support), sellers defend old highs (resistance). Moves often stall there, which is why targets and risk sit near them.

liquidity_check.what: How close price is to a swing level. Entering near a level means a natural place to be proven wrong cheaply, far from any level means risk has no anchor.
liquidity_check.now: Price is {distance_pct}% from the nearest swing level.

volume.what: How much was actually traded. Price says what moved, volume says how many participated. Moves without participation fail more often.
volume.now: Volume is {volume_ratio}x the recent average. {high: 'Above average participation.' | low: 'Below average, weak conviction.'}

funding.what: A periodic fee between traders in futures markets. Positive means the crowd leans long and pays for it, negative means the crowd leans short. Extreme readings show a crowded side.
funding.now: Funding is {funding_pct}% per 8 hours: the crowd leans {crowd_side}.

market_isolation.what: Is this coin moving with the whole market or on its own? A move the entire market shares says less about this specific coin.
market_isolation.now: This coin's daily move is {z_score} standard deviations from the market average: {isolated_or_with}.

trigger_point.what: The price level our formulas identify as the setup's activation point, computed from averages and structure. A calculation, not a recommendation.

risk_level.what: The price at which the setup's logic is invalid, placed beyond structure using the coin's own volatility (ATR). It defines the cost of being wrong before anything else is considered.
risk_level.now: Computed at {risk_price}, {risk_pct}% from the trigger, placed {formula_note}.

target_level.what: The price the setup projects toward, anchored to structure. Together with the risk level it defines the trade's geometry before anyone risks anything.
target_level.now: Computed at {target_price}: {rr_ratio} reward-to-risk against the calculated risk level.

dynamic_risk.what: A risk level that follows price as a move progresses, locking in ground gained instead of staying fixed. It converts open progress into protected progress.
dynamic_risk.now: Currently at {trail_price}, moved from the initial {initial_risk_price}.

r_multiple.what: Results measured in units of the risk taken, not in money. Risking 1 and making 3 is +3R. It makes every outcome comparable regardless of position size.

risk_reward.what: How much the target offers relative to what the risk level costs. A 3:1 setup can be wrong more often than right and still come out ahead.

position_sizing.what: Deciding size from risk, not conviction: choose the loss you accept if the risk level is hit, then derive the size. Shown here for learning, never as instruction.
position_sizing.now: Illustration on your practice capital: risking {risk_pct_capital}% means a {position_size} position for this geometry.

long_short.what: LONG profits if price rises, SHORT profits if price falls. Every setup has a direction, and every check is evaluated relative to that direction.

spike.what: A sudden sharp move, usually on emotion or news. Spikes attract impulse entries, which is exactly when checking structure matters most.
spike.now: This candle moved {spike_pct}% : the kind of move that invites impulse.

fade.what: When a spike runs out of buyers and price slides back down. In a negative regime, our recorded episodes faded before reaching 10% in 7 out of 7 cases.
fade.now: After the spike, price faded {fade_pct}% over {fade_days} days.

squeeze.what: When price keeps pushing against traders who bet on a reversal, forcing them out before any turn happens. Shorting a vertical move without confirmation invites this.
squeeze.now: Price continued {squeeze_pct}% against the impulse short before any weakness appeared.

regime.what: The market's overall state, defined by Bitcoin: bullish (above the 200-day average with a positive weekly), bearish (the opposite), or in transition. The regime decides which directions are even considered.
regime.now: Current regime: {regime_state}. {bear: 'Upward setups are blocked by the mandatory checks.' | bull: 'Downward setups are blocked by the mandatory checks.' | transition: 'The market is between states, conditions rarely align in either direction.'}

regime_transition.what: The messy period when a market changes state. Signals flip on and off, both directions look tempting, and historically this is where accounts get hurt. Recognizing it is a skill.
regime_transition.now: Transition conditions: reversal signals are appearing across multiple coins while the old structure still holds.

scan_snapshot.what: Every scan is a photo of the market at one moment, pulled fresh from the exchange over your connection. The timestamp tells you exactly when.
scan_snapshot.now: This scan reflects the market as of {scan_time}. The score updates only when a daily candle closes.

analysis_lens.what: Chooses which explanation panel accompanies your results: regime, momentum, volume, or full. It changes what is explained, never what passes or the score.
analysis_lens.now: Current lens: {lens_name}. Levels and scores are identical under every lens.

risk_style.what: Adjusts the geometry of the calculated levels: conservative places risk tighter, aggressive gives it more room. It never changes the score or which coins pass.
risk_style.now: Current style: {style_name}. Same setups, different level geometry.

horizon.what: The intended holding window. SWING covers 1 to 7 days and is fully validated. POSITION (weeks and longer) unlocks only when its engine earns validation across market regimes.
horizon.now: Active horizon: SWING. POSITION is in validation: we do not ship what we have not proven.

journal_reveal.what: Every scan is recorded as a scenario, and its outcome is computed later. The result unlocks on your next scan, so learning always closes its loop.
journal_reveal.now: {pending_count} outcome(s) waiting. Your next scan reveals them.

capital_save.what: A scenario where conditions failed, you did not act, and the move went on to lose. The loss you did not take, made visible and counted.
capital_save.now: This save avoided a {avoided_pct}% drawdown scenario.

xp.what: Your professional capital here. Earned for discipline and learning: the first scan of each day, completed lessons, reviewed outcomes. Never for profits, never for volume of activity.

rank.what: Milestones of accumulated discipline: Strategy Apprentice, Risk Manager (1,000 XP), Regime Reader (3,000), Master Strategist (8,000). Ranks unlock knowledge, never signals.

ohlc.what: One candle summarizes one period: where price opened, the highest and lowest it reached, and where it closed. Green closed higher than it opened, red closed lower.
ohlc.now: {date}: open {open}, high {high}, low {low}, close {close}. Daily candle.
